long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Questions involving multiple reaction steps where each step's product becomes the reactant for the next

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions asking 'how many' compounds satisfy a given property, or enumeration of specific cases

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms beyond surface-level recall - intermediates, transition states, electron flow

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties like acidity, basicity, electrophilicity, nucleophilicity, stability, bond strength, etc.

questions.question_text: P is the probability of finding the 1s electron of hydrogen atom in a spherical shell of infinitesimal thickness, dr, at a distance r from the nucleus. The volume of this shell is 4πr²dr. The qualitative sketch of the dependence of P on r is [Graph options]
questions.solution_summary: For 1s orbital, P(r) = (4r²/a0³)e^(-2r/a0). It starts at 0 at r=0, increases to a maximum at r=a0, and then decreases exponentially. The correct graph shows this behavior.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of radial probability distribution for 1s orbital.

questions.question_text: One mole of an ideal gas at 300 K in thermal contact with surroundings expands isothermally from 1.0 L to 2.0 L against a constant pressure of 3.0 atm. In this process, the change in entropy of surrounding (ΔS_surr) in J K⁻¹ is (1 L atm = 101.3 J)
questions.solution_summary: For isothermal process, ΔU = 0. q = −w = P_ext(V₂ − V₁) = 3×101.3 = 303.9 J. ΔS_surr = −q_sys/T = −303.9/300 = −1.013 J K⁻¹.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Entropy change calculation for surroundings.

questions.question_text: The increasing order of atomic radii of the following Group 13 elements is
questions.solution_summary: Due to poor shielding of d-electrons in Ga, its radius decreases below Al. Order: Ga < Al < In < Tl.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Comparison of atomic radii in Group 13.

questions.question_text: Among [Ni(CO)4], [NiCl4]²⁻, [Co(NH3)4Cl2]Cl, Na3[CoF6], Na2O2 and CsO2, the total number of paramagnetic compounds is
questions.solution_summary: Paramagnetic compounds: [NiCl4]²⁻ (Ni²⁺ d8, tetrahedral, 2 unpaired), Na3[CoF6] (Co³⁺ d6, high spin, 4 unpaired), CsO2 (O2⁻ has 1 unpaired). [Ni(CO)4] is diamagnetic (Ni(0) d10), [Co(NH3)4Cl2]Cl is Co³⁺ low spin diamagnetic, Na2O2 is diamagnetic. Total = 3.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of paramagnetic species.

questions.question_text: On complete hydrogenation, natural rubber produces
questions.solution_summary: Natural rubber is cis-1,4-polyisoprene. On complete hydrogenation, it gives poly(ethylene-co-propylene), i.e., ethylene-propylene copolymer.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of natural rubber structure and hydrogenation product.

questions.question_text:
According to the Arrhenius equation,
(A) a high activation energy usually implies a fast reaction.
(B) rate constant increases with increase in temperature. This is due to a greater number of collisions whose energy exceeds the activation energy.
(C) higher the magnitude of activation energy, stronger is the temperature dependence of the rate constant.
(D) the pre-exponential factor is a measure of the rate at which collisions occur, irrespective of their energy.
questions.solution_summary: High activation energy implies slower reaction, not faster (A wrong). Rate increases with temperature because more collisions exceed Ea (B correct). Higher Ea means stronger temperature dependence (C correct). Pre-exponential factor A is collision frequency (D correct).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of Arrhenius equation and its implications.

questions.question_text: A plot of the number of neutrons (N) against the number of protons (P) of stable nuclei exhibits upward deviation from linearity for atomic number, Z > 20. For an unstable nucleus having N/P ratio less than 1, the possible mode(s) of decay is(are)
questions.solution_summary: For N/P < 1, nucleus has excess protons. To increase N/P ratio, it can undergo positron emission (β+) or electron capture (K-capture). Both convert a proton to neutron.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of nuclear stability and decay modes.

questions.question_text:
The crystalline form of borax has
(A) tetranuclear [B4O5(OH)4]²⁻ unit
(B) all boron atoms in the same plane
(C) equal number of sp² and sp³ hybridized boron atoms
(D) one terminal hydroxide per boron atom
questions.solution_summary: Borax has [B4O5(OH)4]²⁻ unit (A correct). It has two sp² and two sp³ boron atoms (C correct). One terminal OH per boron atom (D correct). Not all boron atoms are in same plane (B wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Structure of borax and boron hybridisation.

questions.question_text: The compound(s) with TWO lone pairs of electrons on the central atom is(are)
questions.solution_summary: ClF3: Cl has 5 electron pairs (3 bond + 2 lone) → 2 lone pairs. XeF4: Xe has 6 electron pairs (4 bond + 2 lone) → 2 lone pairs. BrF5 has 1 lone pair, SF4 has 1 lone pair.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: VSEPR theory for lone pair counting.

questions.question_text: The reagent(s) that can selectively precipitate S²⁻ from a mixture of S²⁻ and SO₄²⁻ in aqueous solution is(are)
questions.solution_summary: CuCl₂ gives CuS precipitate (black) with S²⁻, while SO₄²⁻ remains in solution. BaCl₂ would precipitate both S²⁻ (BaS soluble) and SO₄²⁻ (BaSO₄ insoluble). Pb acetate precipitates both. Na₂[Fe(CN)₅NO] gives purple colour with S²⁻ but doesn't precipitate.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of selective precipitation of anions.

questions.question_text: Positive Tollen's test is observed for [Image options]
questions.solution_summary: Tollen's test is given by aldehydes (including aliphatic and aromatic) and α-hydroxy ketones. The compounds in options A and B give positive Tollen's test.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of compounds giving Tollen's test.

questions.question_text: The product(s) of the following reaction sequence is (are) [Image]
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction sequence, the products are options B and C.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step reaction product identification.

questions.question_text: The correct statement(s) about the following reaction sequence is(are) [Image]
questions.solution_summary: Q (salicylaldehyde) gives violet colour with FeCl3 (phenolic). Q is steam volatile due to intramolecular H-bonding. S (benzyl ether of salicylaldehyde) gives yellow precipitate with 2,4-DNP but no FeCl3 colour. So B and C are correct.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step synthesis and functional group tests.

questions.question_text: The mole fraction of a solute in a solution is 0.1. At 298 K, molarity of this solution is the same as its molality. Density of this solution at 298 K is 2.0 g cm⁻³. The ratio of the molecular weights of the solute and solvent, (MW_solute / MW_solvent), is
questions.solution_summary: Let solute be A, solvent B. X_A = 0.1, X_B = 0.9. Molality m = (1000 × X_A)/(M_B × X_B) = 1000 × 0.1/(M_B × 0.9) = 111.11/M_B. Molarity M = (X_A × 1000 × d)/(X_A M_A + X_B M_B) = (0.1×1000×2)/(0.1 M_A + 0.9 M_B) = 200/(0.1 M_A + 0.9 M_B). Given m = M. Solving gives M_A/M_B = 9.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Interconversion of concentration units.

questions.question_text: The diffusion coefficient of an ideal gas is proportional to its mean free path and mean speed. The absolute temperature of an ideal gas is increased 4 times and its pressure is increased 2 times. As a result, the diffusion coefficient of this gas increases x times. The value of x is
questions.solution_summary: D ∝ ℓ × v_avg. ℓ ∝ T/P, v_avg ∝ √T. So D ∝ T^(3/2)/P. When T becomes 4T and P becomes 2P, D₂/D₁ = (4T)^(3/2)/(2P) × P/T^(3/2) = 4^(3/2)/2 = 8/2 = 4.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Dependence of diffusion coefficient on T and P.

questions.question_text: In neutral or faintly alkaline solution, 8 moles of permanganate anion quantitatively oxidize thiosulphate anions to produce X moles of a sulphur containing product. The magnitude of X is
questions.solution_summary: 8MnO₄⁻ + 3S₂O₃²⁻ + H₂O → 8MnO₂ + 6SO₄²⁻ + 2OH⁻. 8 moles MnO₄⁻ produce 6 moles SO₄²⁻. So X = 6.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Redox stoichiometry for permanganate in alkaline medium.

questions.question_text: The number of geometric isomers possible for the complex [CoL₂Cl₂]⁻ (L = H₂NCH₂CH₂O⁻) is
questions.solution_summary: The complex [CoL₂Cl₂]⁻ with two bidentate L ligands and two Cl ligands has 5 geometrical isomers.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Counting geometrical isomers in octahedral complex with bidentate ligands.

questions.question_text: In the following monobromination reaction, the number of possible chiral products is [Image] (1.0 mole) (enantiomerically pure)
questions.solution_summary: Monobromination of the given compound gives 5 chiral products.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Counting chiral products from bromination.

questions.question_text: The correct order of acidity for the following compounds is [Image]
questions.solution_summary: Conjugate base stability determines acidity. Intramolecular H-bonding stabilizes the conjugate base. Order is A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Comparison of acidity of carboxylic acids.

questions.question_text: The geometries of the ammonia complexes of Ni²⁺, Pt²⁺ and Zn²⁺ respectively, are
questions.solution_summary: [Ni(NH3)6]²⁺ is octahedral (Ni²⁺ d8, sp³d²). [Pt(NH3)4]²⁺ is square planar (Pt²⁺ d8, dsp²). [Zn(NH3)4]²⁺ is tetrahedral (Zn²⁺ d10, sp³). So order: octahedral, square planar, tetrahedral.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Geometry of metal ammonia complexes.

questions.question_text: For the following electrochemical cell at 298 K, Pt(s)|H₂(g,1 bar)|H⁺(aq,1 M)|M⁴⁺(aq), M²⁺(aq)|Pt(s). E_cell = 0.092 V when [M²⁺]/[M⁴⁺] = 10^x. Given: E°(M⁴⁺/M²⁺) = 0.151 V; 2.303RT/F = 0.059 V. The value of x is

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Nernst equation.

questions.question_text: The major product of the following reaction sequence is [Image]
questions.solution_summary: The reaction sequence involves aldol condensation followed by Cannizzaro and acetal formation. The major product is option A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step synthesis involving aldol and Cannizzaro.

questions.question_text: In the following reaction sequence in aqueous solution, the species X, Y and Z, respectively, are [Image]
questions.solution_summary: Ag⁺ + 2S₂O₃²⁻ → [Ag(S₂O₃)₂]³⁻ (clear solution). On standing, it gives Ag₂S₂O₃ (white precipitate), which decomposes to Ag₂S (black precipitate). So X=Ag₂S₂O₃, Y=[Ag(S₂O₃)₂]³⁻, Z=Ag₂S.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Reactions of silver with thiosulphate.

questions.question_text: The qualitative sketches I, II and III given below show the variation of surface tension with molar concentration of three different aqueous solutions of KCl, CH3OH and (CH3)3C11H23OSO3Na at room temperature. The correct assignment of the sketches is [Graph options]
questions.solution_summary: KCl is a strong electrolyte → slight increase in surface tension (curve I). CH3OH is an organic compound → decreases surface tension (curve II). Surfactant (detergent) → sharply decreases surface tension (curve III). So I→CH3OH, II→KCl, III→surfactant.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Effect of solutes on surface tension.

questions.question_text: For 'invert sugar', the correct statement(s) is(are) (Given: specific rotations of (+)-sucrose, (+)-maltose, L-(-)-glucose and L-(+)-fructose in aqueous solution are +66°, +140°, -52° and +92° respectively)
questions.solution_summary: Invert sugar is prepared by hydrolysis of sucrose, not maltose (A wrong). It is equimolar mixture of D-(+)-glucose and D-(-)-fructose (B correct). Specific rotation = (+52° - 92°)/2 = -20° (C correct). Bromine water oxidizes glucose to gluconic acid, not saccharic acid (D wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of invert sugar composition and properties.

questions.question_text: Among the following, reaction(s) which gives(give) tert-butyl benzene as the major product is(are) [Image options]
questions.solution_summary: Friedel-Crafts alkylation of benzene with tert-butyl chloride or isobutylene gives tert-butylbenzene. Options A and D give tert-butylbenzene as major product.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of reactions giving tert-butylbenzene.

questions.question_text: Extraction of copper from copper pyrite (CuFeS2) involves
questions.solution_summary: Copper extraction involves: crushing and froth flotation (A correct), removal of iron as slag (B correct), self-reduction to blister copper (C correct). Refining of blister copper is by poling, not carbon reduction (D wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Steps in copper extraction.

questions.question_text: The CORRECT statement(s) for cubic close packed (ccp) three dimensional structure is(are)
questions.solution_summary: Packing efficiency = 74% (B correct). Octahedral voids = 1 per atom, tetrahedral voids = 2 per atom (C correct). Edge length a = 2√2 r (D correct). Nearest neighbours for topmost layer is 9, not 12 (A wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Properties of cubic close packed structure.

questions.question_text: Reagent(s) which can be used to bring about the following transformation is(are) [Image]
questions.solution_summary: The transformation requires selective reduction of carbonyl without affecting ester/epoxide. NaBH4 (C) and Raney Ni/H2 (D) are suitable. LiAlH4 (A) would reduce ester as well. BH3 (B) would reduce carboxylic acid but not ester.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Selective reduction of functional groups.

questions.question_text: Mixture(s) showing positive deviation from Raoult's law at 35°C is (are)
questions.solution_summary: CCl4 + methanol shows positive deviation (A correct). CS2 + acetone shows positive deviation (B correct). Benzene + toluene is ideal (C wrong). Phenol + aniline shows negative deviation (D wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of solutions showing positive deviation.

questions.question_text: The nitrogen containing compound produced in the reaction of HNO3 with P4O10
questions.solution_summary: P4O10 + 4HNO3 → 4HPO3 + 2N2O5. N2O5 is diamagnetic (B correct). N2O5 reacts with Na metal producing NO2 (brown gas) (D correct). It does not contain N-N bond (C wrong). It cannot be prepared by P4 + HNO3 (A wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Properties of N2O5.

questions.question_text: According to Molecular Orbital Theory
questions.solution_summary: C2²⁻ has all electrons paired (diamagnetic) (A correct). O2²⁺ has bond order 3, shorter bond length than O2 (B wrong). N2⁺ and N2⁻ have same bond order (2.5) (C correct). He2⁺ has bond order 0.5, lower energy than two isolated He atoms (D wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: MO theory and molecular properties.

questions.question_text: Thermal decomposition of gaseous X2 to gaseous X at 298 K takes place according to the following equation: X₂(g) ⇌ 2X(g). The standard reaction Gibbs energy, ΔrG°, of this reaction is positive. At the start of the reaction, there is one mole of X₂ and no X. As the reaction proceeds, the number of moles of X formed is given by β. Thus, β_equilibrium is the number of moles of X formed at equilibrium. The reaction is carried out at a constant total pressure of 2 bar. Consider the gases to behave ideally. (Given: R = 0.083 L bar K⁻¹ mol⁻¹). The equilibrium constant Kp for this reaction at 298 K in terms of β_equilibrium is
questions.solution_summary: X₂ ⇌ 2X. Initial moles: 1, 0. At equilibrium: 1−β/2, β. Total moles = 1+β/2. Total pressure = 2 bar. p_X₂ = 2(1−β/2)/(1+β/2), p_X = 2β/(1+β/2). Kp = p_X²/p_X₂ = 8β²/(4−β²).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Calculation of Kp for dissociation reaction.

questions.question_text: Thermal decomposition of gaseous X2 to gaseous X at 298 K takes place according to the following equation: X₂(g) ⇌ 2X(g). The standard reaction Gibbs energy, ΔrG°, of this reaction is positive. The INCORRECT statement among the following, for this reaction is
questions.solution_summary: Decrease in total pressure favours more moles of gas (A correct). At start, dissociation is spontaneous despite ΔG° > 0 because Q < K (B correct). β_equilibrium cannot be determined without ΔG° data (C incorrect). ΔG° > 0 implies K < 1 (D correct).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of equilibrium and Gibbs energy.

questions.question_text: The compound R is [Image options]
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction sequence, compound R is option A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step synthesis product identification.

questions.question_text: The compound T is
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction sequence, compound T is option B.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step synthesis product identification.
